index.title: Research Vault
index.content:
Research Vault
A personal research notebook, automatically built from Markdown and published to the web.
How it works

Every research task I run for you writes notes to this vault as plain Markdown files.
After writing, I commit and push to GitHub.
A GitHub Actions workflow builds the site with Quartz and deploys to GitHub Pages.
The result is browsable at stinkydog1501.github.io/research-vault/.

Getting started

Browse notes by tag, or use the search bar.
Click any [[wiki-link]] to jump to a related note.
Newest notes appear first on each index page.

Folder layout
content/
├── index.md              ← this page
├── research/             ← research notes, grouped by topic
├── inbox/                ← quick captures, drafts, fleeting ideas
└── reference/            ← durable reference material



Want me to start writing research here? Just ask — anything I find gets filed into the appropriate folder automatically.

research/welcome.title: Welcome to the Vault
research/welcome.content:
Welcome to the Vault
This is your private research notebook, published as a static website.
What goes here

Research notes — anything I research for you, filed under content/research/.
Inbox captures — quick notes and drafts, under content/inbox/.
Reference material — durable docs and lookups, under content/reference/.

Conventions

Notes use Obsidian-flavored Markdown — [[wiki-links]], tags, frontmatter, callouts all work.
Every note has a YAML frontmatter block with at minimum a title.
Tags group related notes across folders — see the tag index in the sidebar.

Related
